title: DOJ documents on Charles R. Longo Sr. & Gilbert Sapperstein & tel. Interview of Donald Stone in FL. by MD AUSA George Russell III in MD.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Agreement to Pay Fees & Prioritization Request – 
FOIA EOUSA 2017-000869 (Telephone Interview by AUSA George Russell III)Dear EOUSA FOIA Public Liaison / Kevin Krebs:I am responding to your letter of November 2, 2020, regarding FOIA Request EOUSA 2017-000869 (Third-Party: Gilbert Sapperstein and Charles R. Longo, Sr., both deceased).I hereby agree to pay up to $150.00 in duplication fees. Please proceed immediately with processing and release of responsive records.Specific Record to Prioritize:
Among the responsive records is an extensive telephone interview conducted by then-Assistant U.S. Attorney George L. Russell III (now U.S. District Judge for the District of Maryland) with me (Donald Stone) while I was residing in Florida. This interview directly relates to the matters involving Sapperstein and Longo. I respectfully request that this transcript (and any related notes or memoranda) be located and released to me as a priority, ideally as part of the first 100 free pages or immediately thereafter.To further expedite processing and reduce costs, I narrow my request to the following high-priority items (while still authorizing processing of the full set if needed):The above-referenced telephone interview transcript conducted by AUSA George Russell III;
All investigative reports, FD-302s, prosecution memoranda, declination memos, and correspondence concerning Gilbert Sapperstein and/or Charles R. Longo, Sr.

Because both named third parties are deceased, I request that you apply the most favorable balancing test under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7(C) and release the records with minimal or no redactions where possible.Please notify me of the exact final cost before any charges are incurred and provide a Vaughn index for any withheld or redacted portions.Thank you for your assistance. I can be reached at the contact information below.Sincerely,
Donald Stone
441 A Highland Avenue
Somerville, MA 02144
772 834 6175
ddstonetechnology@gmail.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Don Stone
